relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Full of holy Spirit, Jesus enters wilderness leading.
Forty days of testing meet hunger and human vulnerability.
The first test asks sonship to turn stone into bread.
Jesus answers that human life is more than bread.
The reigns of the inhabited world are shown in a moment.
Authority and glory are offered as compromised power.
The bargain asks worship in exchange for dominion.
Jesus answers with worship and service to the Divine alone.
The test moves to Jerusalem and the temple height.
Scripture is used as bait for spectacle.
Protection promises are twisted into self-display.
Jesus refuses to test the Lord.
The tests pause until another opportune time.
Jesus returns to Galilee in Spirit-power.
His synagogue teaching is received with glory.
In Nazareth, he stands to read on Sabbath.
Isaiah’s scroll is opened to the appointed place.
Spirit anoints him for poor, captives, blind, and oppressed.
The favorable year of the Lord is proclaimed.
The room fixes its eyes on him.
Today the Scripture is fulfilled in their hearing.
Words of favor provoke wonder and familiarity.
They want homeland proof and local display.
No prophet is accepted in the prophet’s homeland.
Elijah’s famine story opens the mercy beyond local claim.
The widow of Sidon receives the prophet.
Naaman the Syrian receives cleansing.
Grace beyond boundary turns wonder into rage.
They drive him toward the edge.
He passes through the middle and goes away.
In Capernaum, he teaches on Sabbaths.
His word carries authority.
A man troubled by an unclean force cries out.
The hidden force recognizes the Holy One.
Jesus commands silence and release without harm.
The people marvel at word with authority and power.
The report spreads through the region.
Jesus enters Simon’s house where fever holds his mother-in-law.
The fever leaves, and service immediately returns.
At sunset, each sick one is touched and healed.
Unclean spirits recognize him, but he silences distorted witness.
Daybreak draws him to a deserted place while crowds try to hold him.
He must announce the good news of the Divine reign to other cities too.
He continues proclaiming in the synagogues.
